Realizing that its attack was futile, the whale shakes its head slightly. Without even glancing at Miss D, it disappears into the spatial fluctuations it had come from.
Whale, my whale... *sob*... Whyyy...
Do you think it's gonna try and ambush us?
Maybe so. We've found it strange ever since Hunter's team last tried to catch it.
When it was cornered, it suddenly showed unexpected intelligence AND weapons we'd never seen before...
Lies! That can't be true!
The whale answers only to me! Only I can control it!
Yes, yes. We can see the truth for ourselves before long.
For now, focus on finding out where that whale went... and how to drag it out of hiding.

Concept Anchoring.say: For the time being, we decide to observe for any more spatial fluctuations – distortions caused by enormous energy sources.
So, Commander... You think it decided to be careful because its ambush failed?
And it'll show up again if we make ourselves look defenseless?
Huh?! That makes sense, but using yourself as bait just sounds too dangerous!
Swordbearer, I agree that patience is key. We still don't know the extent of the whale's power. If we show weakness, it may just prove fatal.
Wanting to be taken advantage of is one thing, but by the enemy? No way!
The whale's goal is to avoid capture, not kill us, right? What if it just hides until we leave?
Th-then what do we do?
What if the whale is so quick to flee because of the recent spatial impact event?
Hmm... Fighting Sirens causes spatial impact events, as I recall. And that leads to spatial fluctuations?
Right. This whole operation began with the idea that the whale is drawn to spatial impact events.
If this place is subjected to the same influence... Hm, this spatial impact event must have been severe.
These coordinates must have been an experimental field where Sirens fought, or something.
But there's nothing here. The spatial impact event was so intense that it wiped the whole area out of existence.
Mmm... Basically, a concept erased from matter returned to another dimension, causing space to fold in on itself and create a special area in that dimension.
For example, the sea where the whale swam. When the sea disappeared, the whale followed the concept of that sea and disappeared from reality.
But once it leaves the world of that concept, that's it. This was our mistake.
What? If there's no physical foundation for it, just make another!
Take a new foundation and anchor the concept to that! Easy peasy!
If that whale gets away now, there's no telling when I'll see it again!
You're right about that. We're in trouble if it escapes into conceptual space again.
At this rate, whoever is manipulating the whale now might just gain full control of it.
In the worst case, this might be our final whale hunt.
I TOLD you, nobody but me can control the whale! When I say you can't, I mean you can't!
But what if that person was you?
You Antiochus can still operate, even when you're incomplete. What if the one controlling the whale is another Miss D?
Ah...
AAAAAAAH!
Damn, damn, damn! That's actually, really possible!
I'm such a moron! I'm gonna get my whale stolen away... by ME!
*sigh*... Well, we're glad you understand the gravity of this situation.
Thank you, Commander. One is sorry you had to calm the two of them...
But one still doesn't understand how we're meant to drag that whale out of hiding.
That makes two of us. I could certainly use more explanation.
I don't get ANY of it!
M-me, either...
Likewise, Swordbearer.
I feel like I kinda got it... Mmm, but I dunno...
It's too complicated for us to explain right now, and you don't need to understand the principles.
However, we have hit upon a way to drag the whale out of hiding. It is dangerous, though.
Our original plan was to snatch up the whale while the Antiochus were busy fighting their enemy...
But the situation implies that their enemy is alive and well, and they aren't hostile toward the whale.
In other words, if we try to drag out the whale, we may be forced to fight two factions at once.
A head-on fight would be hopeless.
So the plan is... for all of you to return to the beta timeline! Miss D and we will figure things out here!
The assistant can't leave!
What?!
I don't know why! But I know that!
We've put up with you for long enough, but our patience is wearing thin.
If you don't give us a proper reason, already, we're taking our servant back home!
Boooo! No means no!
Back me up, assistant! I'll protect you. Just don't leave me!
That's enough! We promised the other Elizabeth that her servant would be returned safe from harm!

options.content: There they go derailing things again...

Concept Anchoring.say: NOOOOOO!
Servant, do you have something you'd like to add?
Assistaaant!
In my opinion, we have too little time to waste it arguing.
I came here well aware of the risks of this operation.
If safety is really our top priority, then now is our chance to give up and go home.
Besides, there is something I want to look into.
Beyond the whale... I can't let slip an opportunity to learn about the Antiochus and the alpha timeline.
As Commander, I've joined in countless battles. Every time we faced mortal danger together, we overcame it.
I trust in the people who protect me, and in return, I want you all to trust in my resolve.
Yeah, there you go! I'll keep you safe, mysterious assistant!
If you say so, Commander... then one is resolved to fight.
Heheh, I won't let anyone lay a finger on you, Commander♥
(I have friends and allies here. Let's think of a countermeasure first before we do anything drastic.)
(If Miss D is willing to ally with us, then we might just be able to get Helena's help.)
(I haven't received any word at all from her since I left the venue.)
(But I know her. Even if our communication lines have recovered, she won't contact me until it's absolutely necessary.)
(Anyway, I can discuss this with everyone another time.)
Your composure betrays a lack of understanding, Commander.
Even we don't want to fight an enemy that can cause such an enormous spatial impact event.
Is it military power that scares you, or the power of the concept?
Both. Do you not believe us?
We trust the Commander. And the Commander trusts you, so...
Alright, then. It seems you're not withdrawing.
As for how to make a foundation to ground the concept and drag out the whale...
The beta timeline had "concept anchors" that they used, yes? In that case...
Elizabeth, the train! Use one of the train cars!
Ugh... Must we really?
Of course! The Queen's Light is strong and dense enough! It's perfect for turning individual space into a foundation!
Urk... But our precious carriage...
(She really looks like she doesn't want to part with this train carriage... It must've taken an astronomical effort to build.)
When we catch the whale, you can just turn it back into a train car!
Ughhh...... Fine.
Now, what concept will we use to manifest the whale?
Perhaps a bridge?
It will come with a river, giving the whale room to swim. If it escapes, we can cross it to give chase.
(I don't like this. It'll just mean leaving everyone behind again.)
Hold on a second. Let's get some things straight.
The whale entered a separate, folded space. That's why we can't access it from the physical world.
So we need to give it a concept and anchor it to it in order to drag it out into our world.
To do this, we use your Mirror Sea train car technology to change a condensed space with the concept of "train" into a different concept, making it into a different space.
If that concept can connect to the space where the whale is, the whale can be manifested here.
And then we capture it before it can escape... Are we straight on that so far?
(Wow... What a fruitful analysis.)
That's more or less correct.
Now, Commander, what do you think of the concept? What's likely to connect us to the space the whale is in?
How about doors?
Maybe we can go through it and go to the whale. There were doors in all of the imaginary spaces connected to the Arbiters... to the alpha timeline, too.
(And I've already been to those spaces several times already.)
(The Commander knows about the doors, too...?)
No, no, nuh-uh! Door and bridge are both wrong!
It's Death! Only Death can capture the whale!
(Death... Huh? Is that what the "D" in Miss D means?)
(Death is a tarot card, too... Is she related to the Arbiters?)
Are you willing to stake our lives on a word that came so easily?
I trust her. It's a little violent, but Miss D knows the whale more than the rest of us combined.
The Commander accepted the concept of death so quickly! It was even faster than this one's analysis module!
The Swordbearer knows more than us. Now is the time to trust our leader's judgment.
I like letting the Commander toy with me. Yeah, I'm just gonna wait and see what happens.
Anchoring the concept of death means, well... We'll have to imagine it.
We will operate the train, so it follows that we shall–
No METAs!
Okay, then let me–
No assistant, either! If you lack the concept, then stay away from it!
I'll pick one of the Iris shipgirls! That's for the best!
...Alsace, you do it!
Hmm? This one should imagine a concept...?
Knowledge on how to do it does not exist in one's memory logs... What now?
Don't worry. It's easy, we'll teach you right now.
First, relax...
Alsace, relax. Alsace, don't be nervous.
Emotion module still running, reducing speed of relaxation below expected value...
Alsace, standby mode!
Understood! Entering standby mode!
...Whoa.
Ahem. That will do. Now, imagine the concept of death.
Not the moment of someone's death, but the manifestation of death, a place emblematic of it... Envision that.
The concept of death. A manifested place, images, imagination...
Keep doing that. The more specific, the better.
Everyone, get on the train. It's almost time to ride.

The Construction and Deconstruction of D.say: We camouflaged the door and Camelot before boarding the Queen's Light.
Boundless stars shine outside the window.
But our destination isn't the distant sea of stars. It's absolute nothingness.
We're almost there. Alsace, are you ready?
M-maybe! Let this one prepare a little longer...
Don't worry. Relax and leave it all to us.
Time stops.
Everything in the carriage halts. Even sound is snuffed out.
Elizabeth alone begins to slowly move.
One step at a time, leaving faint afterimages behind her.
She stops for a moment in front of Alsace. Then, she walks right through her.
Instantly after, time flows anew.
The afterimages left behind by Elizabeth flow through Alsace and melt into their owner.
Our inspection is complete.
Well done. You can stop imagining it now.
It's over already...? Nothing is in this one's memory region...
You did great.
It's a shock that someone can construct the concept of death so well.
Eek, that was like a horror movie brought to life.
Elizabeth just teleported behind Alsace... Like some kind of ghost!
I-I didn't see anything... Nothing at all...
What awesome speed. Even I couldn't keep sight of her.
Hmmm...
Hahaha! Cool, right?
After we catch that whale, I'll be even cooler!
(Huh? Did nobody else see what just happened?)
(Actually, passing through a target so fast that you leave afterimages... I remember the Arbiter we fought in the Mediterranean doing the same thing.)
(She called that... inspection? I'll ask Helena about that.)
So? Did it work, or what?
Nothing looks any different outside.
Yeah... It's all dark and empty...
The train hasn't begun its transformation yet.
Honestly... Maybe we ought to use another car as the foundation for the concept of this lovely view.
Concept conversion, car 7. Become the foundation of death, anchor the unseeable, and manifest its form!

The Martyrium.say: A light erupts from the empty blackness.
Then, golden arches and a pearly white road appear. A circular city emerges from nothingness to everyone's amazement.
It's... beautiful...
Like a Mirror Sea, a compressed space has been freed, birthing an entire city based on a concept.
All this from an object the size of only a train car. It is truly awe-inspiring.
Wooow... I've never seen anything like this before.
This must be how Béarn manipulates space using reality anchors.
Reality anchors? What're those?
They're a reality-stabilizing technology used in Basilicas all the time.
As their name suggests, they can anchor all kinds of concepts. In this city's case, the concept of death.
Béarn would be sooo excited if she could see this. Shame she's not here.
(This doesn't really FEEL like a city based on the idea of death, though. It more brings to mind images of brilliance and holiness.)
(Is it down to interpretation? Is this how Alsace views death?)
Alsace, does this city have, like, a name?
Hm? Not that one knows of.
A splendid structure of gold and marble to commemorate death...
This brings to mind an idea – the Martyrium. How does that sound for a name?
The Martyrium... A shrine built atop a grave, yes? One approves of this idea!
It's a good name. Let's call it that.
While you likely aren't conscious of it, it is good to name things according to your concept of them.
If others accept the concept, it then... Bah, never mind that.
A small problem has cropped up.
We're unable to anchor the concept properly. The space the whale is occupying won't fully manifest in reality.
Our spatial impact event was strong, but the amount of data within the folded space is simply mind-boggling... Even a whole train car could only unfold this much.
With a booming rumble, the whale suddenly appears in the sky above the Martyrium.
The whale! My whale is here!
Oh. So that was still all it took to lure it out.
Elizabeth, start the train! We've got to catch my whale!
Don't be so hasty. The Martyrium is a stand-alone space akin to a Mirror Sea. We cannot catch the whale without entering the space.
Then do it! Go to the city! Start the train NOW!
You can be such a pest... Fine, starting the engine now.
...Wait. The whale isn't alone.
What do you mean? There are others?
METAs, to be precise. Two groups, one of which is uncamouflaged. Wichita and Kimberly.
Judging by this duo... it's the Ashes.
Even all the way out here, we're still running into them...
(But, one of our own is working with them – Friedrich. Maybe we can ask them about Enterprise, or Hiryuu.)
The other group is camouflaged. We can't identify them at this range.
Only we know about the whale, so they're most likely here only to investigate the spatial impact event.
As if we didn't have enough to deal with...
They WILL know about the whale as soon as they look up!
Start the damn train already! Or they'll get to it first!
STOP! RUSHING! US! Furthermore, we don't know who we're up against! Rushing in blindly will be our death!
For now, everyone will stay put inside the train. We shall go and scope things out.
The Queen's Light's anti-conceptual defences are impregnable. You'll be safe here while we're out.
Commander, I've heard you're an excellent babysitter. Watch this sassy child in our absence! Make sure she doesn't run off.
You got it.
Why would I run away from the assistant? Just go and come back quick!

Ashen Plans.say: Sometime earlier...
Is this it, Kimberly? Are we at the origin point of the spatial distortion?
Soon – we're close. It's strange, though... there are no wreckages to be seen here.
According to theory, there should be loads of them.
I've not seen ANYTHING, for that matter. The whole branch has literally been erased.
There's only one explanation.
The impact released so much energy that the space completely lost stability.
It wiped away the real-world foundation of the entire branch and the test site with it, folding it into a higher dimension. Would explain why we can't see squat from outside.
Simple. Just cause another spatial impact to create a pathway.
My thoughts exactly. Good thing I have a machine that can do just that.
Lead the way. I've got a real fireworks show for ya.
As the two Ashes were drafting a plan, they saw the light of the Martyrium on the horizon.
...Wichita?
I see it.
Someone rebuilt the foundation with a conceptual anchor, unfolding the space. Guess that's one way of doing it.
Whoever did it is hiding themselves.
They're doing it damn well, too. My radar's got nothing.
Forget about that city, though – see the whale in the sky? You recognize it?
Arbiter XIII... Death.
She's here. Maybe even to do the same thing as we are.
Either way, let's hurry. We gotta kill the target first, or we'll have wasted our time coming here.
And ignore whoever rebuilt the foundation?
Don't give a damn. We'll find 'em sooner or later.
If they're friendly, we'll talk it out. If they're not, we'll duke it out. Either way, they're not stopping us!

The Third Party.say: Meanwhile, elsewhere...
Well, well... We just got here, and already we're seeing a spectacle.
A conceptual anchor. Impressive. Do you have any guess as to whose, Devil?
Not a single clue. They've perfectly concealed themselves.
All I know is that they smell like they're from test site beta.
You mean the branch where Pamiat' ran into big trouble? Interesting.
How can you tell?
Arbiters have their secrets, too.
Fine. And the whale flying in the sky? What the hell is that about?
What, you don't know?
The things I don't know could fill your whole database.
You realized I only just escaped that place, yes? For now, all I can see are glimpses of things.
Hahah. Okay, I'll tell you. That thing is a huge deal.
And I mean huge. You'll be set for life if you can capture it.
Uh-huh. Are you going to tell me what it is or not?
Nope. If you don't know, I'm not obligated to tell you. I'm not your mom.
Buuut, I've heard that "They" are looking for it, and will grant a hefty reward for its return.
Really? Then I have to get my hands on it.
No, wait, first I need to know the concept tied to that foundation. I will not be a fool and rush in blindly.
You don't recognize the concept? I do.
It's Death.

sequence: Mirror Rail "Queen's Light"
Car 5
Present Time

Encroaching Threat.say: 15 minutes have passed since Elizabeth left the train. We who stayed behind are lounging around as we wait for her to return.
Miss D threw a tantrum earlier, lasting 5 minutes. Now she's just sitting quietly in a corner.
Meanwhile, some of us are idly gazing out the windows at the mysterious city.
Building such a splendid city simply by picturing it. Can you imagine that?
We are all blessed to have witnessed this.
While we're here, we should take a picture!
Hang on, lemme find my camera...
If you wish to record it for posterity, one has already done so and is preparing to compile a report for the Cardinal.
No, not for posterity! For us! I'm talking about taking a picture as a keepsake!
One has... questions about the practical purposes of doing so.
It's to show other people and say, "Look! We were here!"
Like, we're the only ones who'll ever come here. They'll be so jelly when they see a picture of how pretty this place is!
Uh-huh... Thought processor RPM is rapidly increasing...
Fl-Fleur, please don't mess with her like that...
I'm serious here! Don't you wanna show a pic to Tem and Opie and see the looks on their faces?
All I want is to go home and be alone.
Oh. Alrighty then.
Brrrrr...
What's wrong, Miss D?
Miss D has huddled up in her little corner and started shaking like a leaf.
Cold... It's so cold in here! Don't you feel it? It's freezing!
Uh... Not really?
One's body temperature remains stable.
Should I, um, get you a blanket?
No! There's an intruder! Someone's snuck aboard the train!
That's why it's so cold! I'm freezing my butt off!
*sniff sniff*... Yeah, I'm smelling something, and it's not the Commander...
It's corrupt and damp, the stench of depressing death...
One's olfactory system isn't detecting anything. Could it be... the system is malfunctioning?
I guess it's not strictly a smell, more like an aura. I've worked as a Tribunal judge for a long time, so I've developed a keen sense for these things.
Didn't Elizabeth say the train's conceptual defenses are impregnable?
Although, if both you and Miss D sense something is wrong, I don't think that's a coincidence.
Nobody panic, alright? Let's contact Elizabeth before anything else.
The assistant is right! Call her!
We double-checked. There is no sign of any intruder.
There IS an intruder! They're getting closer and closer!
They're only 1 car away! The barriers between them are useless! Heeelp!
Alright, we trust your intuition.
What we don't understand is how this intruder could break in without leaving a single trace along the way...
No matter. Gather everyone in a single car and we'll be right there.
Incidentally, we identified the other group that arrived here. One of them is an Arbiter – The Devil XIV. You surely know all about her.
The other, it seems, is a META. We haven't yet figured out who, though.
Hang in there. We're coming.
(Devil? And a META is accompanying her?)
(That's really bad news. We need to assume a defensive formation...)
Hm? Commander, do you want us to prepare a defense?
Yeah. You can smell where the intruder is coming from, right?
Sure can!
Mogador points at the door connecting to the next train car.
I thiiink the best strategy is if me, Alsace, and Brennus go to the next car and intercept the intruder while the rest of you just stay here.
(On one hand, it divides our force. On the other, it gives them more room to fight and guarantees we have an escape route.)
Let's do that. Everyone else, keep an eye out for the enemy!

The Faker.say: Alsace, Mogador, and Brennus leave the train car while the rest of us stay behind.
Little by little, I can feel the approaching hostile presence become palpable.
Commandeeer! We've entered car 3 and I can see something.
There's this... black mist coiling around, and some kind of white thing floating in the air.
Hoo boy, they have an intense presence. I see why Miss D was terrified now.
If fear had a smell, this place would reek of it.
The sound of death drawing near. A quiet yet intense noise...
I'm going to temporarily deactivate my emotion module. That should make me, uh, not afraid!
Despite the considerable distance between us, the aura of death is clear as day.
An inexpressible pressure leaks through the cracks in the door, acting on my body as though it were a physical thing.
(Rotting and damp – the stench of overwhelming death. Yeah, Mogador, you hit the nail on the head with those words.)
I'm scared! I'm scaaared!
No, no, nooo! How did something so terrifying get inside the train?!
I'm not scared, I'm not scared, I'm not scared...
My friends are here, so I'm not scared...
Don't kid yourself! You're trembling as much as anyone else!
I-I can't help it... I'm so scared I feel like I could die!
Me toooooo!
Whatever's coming, it's bad. BAD.
Don't know how long I can hold out against it... but I'm not giving up without a fight.
Commander, fall back to car 6! I'll hold the line somehow!
(This is getting dire already...)
Hey, Commander, do we start attacking yet?
(I don't doubt their combat ability, it's just that we'll suffer serious casualties if we end up picking a fight with an Arbiter.)
(But what's the alternative? There are only so many cars left to retreat to...)
Umm, the enemy has entered car 4. They'll get the first strike in if we don't do something now.
I look through the glass of the connecting door. There, in car 4, I see it – something floating in the air, covered in a black mist, coming straight toward me.
Hahh...
(God dammit. Better to do SOMETHING than to just sit here.)
Mogador! Open fi–
Wait! Stop, stop, stop!
Just before I can issue the order, Miss D jumps in front of me and interrupts me.
I know now! I know who the intruder is!
A faker! She's a faker!
A faker, faker, faker!
She's a stupid darn faker!
Calm down. What do you mean, "faker"?
It's all her fault I couldn't catch the whale! And she only got this far because she's a faker!
She didn't force her way inside the train! She imitated my whole concept and fooled her way in!
She... imitated your concept? So she's, like, the same as you?
So if you're an Arbiter, that means this enemy is one, too... But what's her name?
Who cares about her name?! What matters is you'll die if you fought me, and you'll die if you fight her!
Get out of there! Ruuun!
We cannot abandon the Swordbearer's mission. Furthermore, what solution is running away?
If we can't stop her, we'll have to abandon the train entirely...
That's what I'm saying! Screw the train and go to the Martyrium!
No, you can't be serious! How would we even go there in the first place?
One could materialize her rigging for you all to ride... However, the navigability of this space is highly uncertain.
Without air, one's engines cannot run – moreover, the Commander will asphyxiate.
Don't be dumb! I'm not talking about going out THERE!
The Martyrium was manifested using the 7th car, remember? It has to be connected to the 6th car!
We'll go through the door and end up there! Come on, get back here now!
Wait, that's how it works?
One's thought processor is beginning to overheat...
That sounds like a stretch, but maybe she's right?
There's no time to argue! Assistant, tell them to retreat NOW! Do it, do it, do it!
If the car connects straight to the city, why didn't Elizabeth just use that route?
Because she's a stupid moron and you're not!
Fleuret, go to car 4 and pick up Alsace's team! We'll link up in car 6 and retreat through the door to the Martyrium!
There we go! Run! Run, everybody!

Emergency Escape.say: Holding Miss D's hand, I step through the door into the 7th car. Suddenly, I am thrust into nothingness.
My sense of time, space, and even self all become hazy, and it's like a mighty force is pulling me apart.
Thought by thought, it feels like my mind is becoming one with this endless void.
Have you ever thought about death? About what it means to these girls? It is neither an end, nor a release for them.
Just as it feels like I'll disappear, a familiar voice speaks to me.
For better or for worse, they will never truly die as long as they're remembered by people.
Death, furthermore, is not a one-way trip for them as it is for us.
"Dead" and "living" are no more than states of being to them.
Every time they live, and every time they die, a little more information is simply added to their names.
From a dialectic perspective, death is neither good nor bad for them. It's a neutral concept.
Remember this, it's important – they form a collective of countless fragments of data until they are given individual bodies.
It is possible to replicate the individual offshoots of this collective. I gave this power to Death.
...Hey! Don't just stand there!
(That was Miss D's voice... but something's off. Is she not aware of what she just said?)
Hey! Moron! Way to go getting separated and freaking everyone out!
...What? You thought I brought you here?
No, obviously not! Why'd you even come here? You'll literally fall apart if you stay any longer!
Take my hand! I'll get you out!
I grab hold of Miss D's hand. The blank void vanishes with a snap, giving way to golden light.
I stand in a city carved from marble, arches of gold spanning overhead. The scent of the Iris countryside fills the air, as does faint, serene cathedral music.
(This is the Martyrium... I made it.)
Thank heavens you're okay, Commander! You just disappeared on us, and I thought you were gone...
Hah, you're naive! Nobody goes gone if I decide so!
Another miracle to the Swordbearer's name. It's a relief that you're unharmed.
Sorry if I scared you all. I just got a bit lost.
"A bit" lost! You're the ONLY one who did! You're such a moron!
Such insolence! You know, one's databanks say that those who call others morons are usually morons themselves!
Sure, moron! I only call morons morons!
Lies! One would never accept that the Commander is a moron!
Ugh... What is this, the playground? Stop arguing like a pair of kids.
They'll tire eventually. More importantly, where'd the intruder go?
I think we shook her off.
I love getting off– I mean, shaking off an enemy.
Also, now we know concepts can ignore things like, you know, physical distance between two points in space...
(Technically, we're in a Mirror Sea, so maybe that's how this miracle was possible. I wonder if the Sirens' teleporters and artificial fog function in a similar vein...)
Ugh... Whatever! The assistant ISN'T a moron! There! Happy now?
One has won this argument!
Alsace triumphantly places her hands on her hips and basks in her strategic victory.
Good. Now, Miss D, will that enemy come after us?
"That enemy"... I hate how that sounds! She needs a name!
Something like... I know! Death's Shadow!
Yeah! I don't care what she thinks, we're gonna call her that!
(Is this another case of "the importance of a concept"?)
Okay. Is Death's Shadow going to come after us?
Anything I can do, she can do! Which means yeah, she will! Unless she's a total moron!
But forget about her, there's something more important! We've reached the Martyrium, remember?
And what are we here to do? Catch the whale! Whale tiiime!
Miss D points a finger at the whale swimming through the sky as she jumps around ecstatically.
But, before we can catch the whale...
What?! The enemy breached the train's defences because their concepts are one and the same?!
That does explain why we couldn't detect an intrusion from far away... The fact we can't detect her now either likely means she moved elsewhere.
We just got back inside the train. Needless to say, there is no trace of... "Death's Shadow" to be found anywhere.
Elizabeth, Miss D called you a moron earlier for not using the direct route through the 6th car's door.
Did she, now? No sweets for her for a month.
Why?!
Did you consider WHY we didn't use that route? Because it significantly raises the risk of a pursuer following us into the train.
Oh...
Yes, now you know. Stay put and we'll be there shortly.
No, don't come here! You weren't supposed to be here to begin with!
Come again? So what is your plan – to confront Devil without us?
Nuh-uh! No confrontations! I'm gonna catch the whale then bail!
We can't let it get away again! You've gotta stay there and make the Mirror Sea and the concept stable!
Make it super-duper stable! Then it will be trapped in here!
Out of the question! We'll run out of resources if we make it any stabler!
Or what, are you seriously telling us to use ANOTHER train car?!
You can always turn it back! If the whale gets away now, who knows if we'll ever catch it!
Tsk. So be it. Nothing ventured, nothing gained, we suppose.
I'm with Miss D on this. Please do it.
Very well. We shall support you as best as we can from the train.
In the worst-case scenario, we'll come and rescue you. Now, off you go. Enjoy the Martyrium.
You heard her, everybody. Let's go catch that whale!

Words, Not Swords.say: With Elizabeth's cooperation, our team starts heading toward the whale.
At first, we walked, until we realized that the "clouds" around us had identical properties to water. So the girls deployed their riggings.
We should be able to reach the whale by traveling from cloud to cloud.
Before we do that, though, there's an issue that needs to be resolved.
*sniff sniff*... The Commander was right. Somebody's up ahead.
What? But one's sensors aren't detecting anything.
I think they're using cloaking technology...
Yeah. That's the Ashes' modus operandi.
Signals detected! They are approaching from opposite directions! Requesting orders!
(Considering our previous encounters, they probably just want to talk.)
Let's see if we can solve this with words. Stay on your guard, but don't fire the first shot.
I'll try to reason with them.
The Ashes acknowledge our non-hostile intent and turn off their cloaking devices, showing themselves.
I'm Wichita, this is Kimberly. We're with the Ashes. You don't look the least bit surprised to see us.
No. We knew you were here before we even set foot in the Martyrium.
Set foot in the what now? Oh, you mean this place. You created it, didn't you?
You could say that.
I want to know who exactly. Which one of you did it?
I'm afraid I can't tell you. Let's change the topic. What do you say to a cease-fire while we're here?
Why?
I know your Hiryuu and Ark Royal. I've fought side by side with them. Friedrich from our timeline is also working with you.
Ahh. So you're the people from test site beta.
You're test site beta? That explains a lot.
We know all about you. Had a big battle a while back, didn't you?
(Big battle? Is she talking about the near-superimposition event? They know more than they let on.)
(Assuming Friedrich has been feeding them intel, maybe I can get these two to agree to a temporary alliance.)
I appreciate what you've done for Friedrich. How's she been holding up?
Just fine. She's working on something big back at our hideout.
You sure seem to know a lot. I'm guessing you're the Commander I've heard so much about.
In the flesh.
Figures. Hiryuu was right – just seeing ya brings back fond memories.
Know what I mean, Kimberly?
Yes. I'm... reminded, too.
A lot has changed since you and Enterprise met. Been making some odd decisions lately. Her letting in Friedrich was one of 'em.
Have to admit, though, she's been doing good.
Tell ya what, I'll bite – who are you, exactly?
Good question. You're probably familiar with my future self, whereas I'm not. I'd like to know more.
In fact, that's why I'm here. To find out more.
I've already told you who you are! You're the mysterious assistant!
Never heard of a "mysterious assistant" before. Ring any bells, Kimberly?
Not sure. If I have any memories of such a person, they're buried deep.
I'll ask Enterprise later back at the base. You there, the shortie – how do you know the "assistant"?
I have a name! It's Miss D!
Sure ain't a name I've ever heard before. You got something to hide?
None of your business! All you need to know is my name is Miss D!
I don't trust that rat. She's obviously a damn Antiochus! Bet she's a spy who's infiltrated test site beta!
YOU'RE a spy!
Ya hear that? She didn't deny being an Antiochus!
She's an Arbiter. Arbiter XIII: Death, to be precise.
Bullshit. I've fought Death more times than I can remember and that sure ain't her.
Ya see that whale in the sky? THAT'S Death.
Huh?! You're a big fat moron! The faker duped you, moron!
Call me a moron again and you're getting it, you little shit.
Eek!
She's just a kid. Don't threaten her.
Sure, she's a kid, and kids need to be put in their place.
Anyway... If that half-pint is with you people, that changes things. We'll leave the Antiochus alone as long as she doesn't mess with us.
We've still gotta do something about that spatial impact event, though. Just hope we won't have to fight each other over it.
That's what you came here for, right? To measure it?
In a way. Our goal here is to catch the whale. Getting data on the event is just a side objective.
Really, huh? We didn't know about the whale 'til we got here. Guessing you knew about it from the start.
That thing is a giant database with fins, you know.
Interesting. So, what's your stance on us working together on this?
There's an Arbiter – Devil – somewhere in this place, and I don't think she's just a vessel. She's our enemy, and yours as well.
Speak of the devil, as they say... Yeah, we detected her as soon as we stepped inside the... "Martyrium."
But here's the thing. We work best alone.
Best I can do is agree to keep out of your hair if you keep out of ours.
(I almost forgot the Ashes is filled with lone wolves. If that's how they want it...)
That's fine by me. Good luck on your task.
Yeah. Same to you.

The Second Ring.say: The Ashes and we go our separate ways, and we resume our climb toward the whale.
However, we soon see that someone has gotten to the whale before both us and the Ashes.
KABOOOM!
The whale is being attacked by what appears to be drones!
It's a sizable swarm as well, assuming the form of a net to catch it.
Those things look familiar... Oh, I remember! They're Devil's drones!
Nooooo! Devil, you big moron! Be gentle! You're gonna kill my whale!
No, wait, it's gonna run away before anything else!
See?! It's already getting ready to leave!
...Wait, what?! Great! The faker's gotten her hands on a new weapon!
As the whale is surrounded by drones, its body suddenly begins to glow blue.
Every drone touched by the light swiftly loses control and falls to the ground. The whale then turns its dorsal fin and starts swimming for the Martyrium's center at speed.
It reaches the limit of the city's ring, touching the boundary. A spatial distortion occurs, and the whale once more slips off into space.
Dammit. It got away again.
Elizabeth! Pick up, Elizabeth!
Use your train to expand the Martyrium! Draw the whale toward us!
Uggghhh...
Concept conversion, car 6. Broaden the horizon of death, materialise the unseeable, and manifest its form!
A radiant light burst forth from the void once more.
The Mirror Sea's concept is reinforced, expanding the Martyrium with a second ring furnished with gold and marble and arches just like the first.
There, in the air above the second ring, the whale appears again.
The Martyrium grew even bigger and brighter than before!
Let's see that whale try to run away NOW!
Incredible. We've unfolded so much space, and yet there's still more...
One wonders what terrifying creature caused such a massive spatial distortion...
Doesn't matter! All that matters is that it doesn't get away again!
You can't let it happen a third time!
We said we'll do it, remember? Believe us, we won't let it flee.
Thank you! You're nice, Elizabeth!
Come on, assistant! We've got to get back to catching the whale!
How, though? There's no connecting path to the new ring.
We weren't exactly built for jumping across clouds, so...
Not that you could, anyway. Just like how each train car is independent of the next, the rings are independent of each other.
Remember how certain Mirror Seas have rules for entering or exiting them? These work in a similar way.
In short, the method that worked on the first ring won't work on the second. We must find a new way inside.
It's fine! The train cars are connected, so we can go in the same way as we did last time!
Just come with me and we'll get there! Don't worry about the conceptual rule!
We should at least know the rules before we start ignoring them. What's the conceptual rule we're dealing with?
What do you think? Death! Death is the whole shtick of this place, remember?
Like I said, don't worry about it! Just come along!
The whale isn't gonna wait for us! Hurry!

The Rules of the Space.say: Somewhere, in the Martyrium...
KABOOOM!
Impressive... I recover and reinforce my body more and more, and every time you knock me down regardless.
You're no ordinary Ashes. You're two of its founding members, are you not?
Inhibiting your fear of death and instantly reviving... Just a bunch of tricks.
I've seen people like you do it a million times.
I've seen how their minds deteriorate 'til they're just one step away from losing it completely.
So how the hell have you kept your sanity?
Just who the hell are you?
KABOOOM!
Blathering away even as you barrage me... You really are a seasoned warrior.
Sadly for you, my secrets are staying with me.
Look at that new ring. They've anchored twice now, creating two Mirror Seas, and still that which is hidden hasn't been revealed.
Whoever caused this spatial fluctuation wields unimaginable power. How I wish I could meet them.
KABOOOM!
You must think you're real hot shit, looking away during a battle with the Ashes.
I told ya – I've seen your tricks a million times. You think you're invincible?
I'm not, and besides, death is only another way to the next destination.
Haven't you realized what concept rules this space and what rules are woven through it?
Ahahah! Haven't even thought about 'em until now.
Been a long while since I met someone with your take on death. The Antiochus must really love test site beta.
It's not that I laugh in the face of death. Death is a prerequisite to entering the second Mirror Sea.
Death is the concept that rules this space. It is where all living things end up.
Moreover, the very concept of death has been redefined here. You literally cannot kill me, no matter what you do.
I don't need to kill you. I can just tear you to shreds.
Even if you won't die, your wounds ain't going anywhere and you'll eventually run out of energy.
Maybe I can't reduce you to dust – or maybe I can. Only one way to find out.
KABOOOM!
Your birth was never meant to be. Perish.
Now you fancy yourselves the arbiters of who gets to be born, do you?
Tell me, how many people have you killed?
All those souls, snuffed out – did those deaths get you what you wanted?
Shut up. Every little rat like yourself dead is one less problem for the universe.
Don't think I don't know where your power comes from, who you're colluding with, and what you're planning.
Scum like you don't get to preach morality!
KABOOOM!
I'd hoped to make you doubt yourselves, but alas...
You leave me no choice but to grant you your wish.
Our final destination may be the same, but I'll give you a small nudge to get there!
Somewhere, in the Martyrium...
Two types of rings... A city?
A new area has appeared. Fancy that.
Assuming that death is the requirement for entry...
––I guess I'll just die.
KABOOOM!
Away from the Martyrium, between the gate and the train...
Inside what can only be called a battleship, a blue-haired girl stared at a monitor, a gentle expression on her face.
I wasn't expecting those meddlers to be THIS powerful.
I had to call on the Tower's power to finally get here...
And now, they've just stopped.
Maybe they're afraid to face me. I hope so, at least.
Whoever you are, wherever you are...
I will find you and destroy you for hunting the Commander and getting between us!
Glowing blue fragments shot through the air, lighting up the vast interior of the ship.
70% of the matter folded up by the spatial impact event has materialized, divided across two Mirror Seas...
Looks like I'm late, but not TOO late.
The Commander is riiight... there.
Oh? Wichita and Kimberly are here, too?
Change of plans. Abort the recycling process and engage the overwrite. Initiate plan E.
Helena, exiting the ship now.

The Answer to All.say: I'm here again...
I grabbed Miss D's hand and was sent back to the void again.
It stretches on endlessly in all directions. Time and orientation mean nothing here.
Unlike last time, however, my consciousness isn't being wrestled from me. It's like I'm in a protective bubble, augmenting my perception of my surroundings rather than weakening it.
This raised awareness lets me detect something amongst the nothingness.
Embracing death, one after the next... Is this what you chose?
Then "I" made the correct choice.
A woman's voice devoid of emotion. Close, yet distant, coming closer, yet getting further away.
Someone is speaking to me through the darkness.
Life comes from nothing, and to nothing death brings it back.
Oblivion is the answer to all.
You would be wise to accept your demise.
It's better than fighting in vain somewhere between the beginning and the end.
(She can't be far, but I have no idea where she is.)
(All I know is that she's watching us... Watching the Martyrium.)
(Who is she? And what is this place?)
Really?
Then what if death didn't mean returning to nothingness?
(Is that... Miss D? She sounds older, somehow.)
What...?
You can hear "my" voice?
Who are you?
Mmgh... *gasp*! No, who are YOU? What are you doing with the assistant?!
"Assistant"?
You heard me! Assistant, why are you here AGAIN? I'm telling you, you WILL get torn apart!
Miss D points to where I am. This finally alerts the other person to my presence.

options.content: I wasn't trying to eavesdrop, I swear.

The Answer to All.say: Someone else... is here?
Who are you people? Why have you come here?
Long story short–
Shut up, you! There's no time to talk!
Don't make me repeat how dangerous this place is! Come along!
And you, whoever you are! You coming with us?
"Me"?
No, "I'm" afraid "I'm" staying here.
What do you mean, "no"? Do you LIKE being in this dark, dreary place? Suit yourself, weirdo!
Assistant, we're going back to the Martyrium! Hang on tight and don't get lost a third time!

The Sun Sets on Samos.say: As the light recedes, I find myself standing on a beach at sunset.
(What the hell is this place? Wasn't she bringing me back to the Martyrium?)
One has spotted the targets! Miss D and the Commander are uninjured!
You had me worried, Swordbearer. You lagged behind us again.

options.content: If this isn't the Martyrium, what is it?

The Sun Sets on Samos.say: This ONLY happens with you every time we warp! If you're doing it on purpose, cut it out!
I have no clue, sorry. We just moved on forward, and the next thing I know, here I am.
All I can tell you is it's an island with some decently-sized coral reefs.
Hey, Alsace – you sure this place didn't come from your imagination?
One never imagined any sort of island, let alone this particular one!
Okay, then why are we here? Did you make a wrong turn or something, Miss D?
I don't make mistakes, full stop! Tell them, Elizabeth!
Miss D did not botch the jump. Your coordinates are, in fact, inside the Martyrium.
Specifically, you're in the second ring that we manifested using our sixth train car. The concept that rules there is still death.
You're still with us, Elizabeth! I thought our comms would be down.
Your phrasing suggests this is a regular occurrence for you.
Worry not. That will not happen as long as we're backing you up.

options.content: Thanks. I'm glad we have you.
Thank you, Your Majesty. META or not, you can always be counted on!

The Sun Sets on Samos.say: (The improved communicator probably helps, too. In any event, I can better deal with emergencies this way.)
Yes, and you should be grateful for it!
Are you SURE this is the Martyrium? It looks nothing like the outside.
The second ring looked, you know, just like the first one. Gold, marble, yadda yadda.
Because the inside isn't the outside! It's a stand-alone space, just like the train cars!
Does it also confuse you how those look different on the inside, or what?
But these two are COMPLETELY different! A train's interior still looks like a train's interior even if you redecorate it!
I mean... Yeah, I guess two manifestations of the concept of death shouldn't look THIS different.
I've got it! The concept probably got all jumbled up 'cause there's too many people here!
This isn't JUST Alsace's idea of death – it's mashed together with someone else's, too. Several people's, in fact!
That's the only explanation for why it looks so different!
(Then if this is a mix of concepts, does that mean it's closer to what the space originally looked like?)
Hang on... It doesn't just LOOK different, it functions differently, too!
Time is flowing in here!
Uh, isn't time a constant? Because it doesn't really start or stop?
I'm not talking about OUR time, moron! I mean time is flowing for this space!
Elizabeth didn't make it like that when she materialized the Martyrium.
She didn't, because all we needed was a simple foundation. She made an unchanging space!
But this place is different. The concept of time has been added to it.
That means this isn't a representation of static death anymore – it represents fluid death!
Come again? So, like... this space will change as time goes on?
Yeah! Exactly that!
Which means I have no idea what's coming!
Be that as it may, death is still death. Whatever will happen, it will involve death.
You're not wrong!
(So it's like a videotape? A tape showing the process of someone dying... Or maybe, of this world dying.)
Waves lapping at the beach. Tropical plants up on a small hill. It looks like any old island across the Pacific.
However, one thing sets it apart – the building off in the distance.
(The exterior is a little different from how I remember it, but the silhouette and location match...)
(That's it. It's the Samos Marine Research Institute from the alpha timeline!)
(If my guess is right, then this is a recording of the death of someone from that timeline – or of the "death" of this space.)
Th-this place creeps me out...
It looks so calm and peaceful, but death is around the corner...
You needn't be so afraid. Death is merely the rule that governs that space and the means to get in, out, and around it.
Dying in there, so to speak, does not spell your literal death. That would only be a concern if the concept were to engulf you.
Apropos of nothing, we checked again and have determined that there is STILL more space to unfold, if you can believe that.
Know that if you need to pursue the whale further, you can use death to get to the next space.
A-are you suggesting actually having a brush with death?
One would much prefer to travel using the train cars!
Death won't mess with you as long as I'm here. Besides, I have a better idea!
We'll just catch the whale right here and now!
Great idea! Only problem is, I don't see the whale anywhere. It's probably run off again.
Nuh-uh! I know it hasn't! It's just hiding somewhere nearby!
Somewhere like... Aha! The sea!
That's GOT to be where it is!
Ready or not, we're going swimming!
Just a second, Miss D. Don't you recognize this island? And more importantly, that building over there?
I point to the research institute for her.
(If she's an Arbiter, she has to know the place Dr. Aoste did his research.)
Oh, you mean the Marine Research Institute?
Yeah, I remember it. I think it was my house. Or yours. Or both of ours. Maybe.
"Maybe"?
I'm not sure, okay?! It's just a gut feeling!
All my important memories are stored inside the whale! I won't remember until we catch it!
How come YOU remember being here, anyway?
"Remember" isn't really the right word. It's too much to explain right now. Can we just please investigate the institute first?
Wait, you recognize this place?
I do. It's Samos Island, located in the Bermuda Sea. Well, Samos Island from the future.
From the alpha timeline? You mean to say that this space was built with data from there?
Fiiine, we'll do as you say, assistant. We'll investigate the institute and THEN go catch the whale!

Laplace's Demon.say: We walk to the institute, my memory of the route guiding us.
It's the same place I went in the Reality Lens and the same place Kronshtadt and her team described, yet stark differences remain.
There are no shops, no stalls, and no facilities catering to tourists. They've all been replaced by defensive installations and barricades.
The mask of peace has come off, revealing the island's true nature as a military base.
All of a sudden, sirens start to blare.
An alarm. Who carelessly tripped the security system?
One's sensors are detecting nothing... They are working, before you ask.
Wasn't me. I'm the one who's been switching off all the systems.
Since when? I had no idea you were doing that!
You Tribunal people scare me...
I don't think any of us tripped it. The alarms are going off across the whole island, and I don't think they activated just to arrest us.
But then who did? One does not understand.
An enemy fleet has appeared 50 nautical miles northwest. It seems they're heading your way.
A fleet? Can you ID them?
They're corrosion entities.
You mean those things that the black tornadoes spew out and cannot be analyzed?
If you mean corrosion phenomena, yes, but they can manifest through other means.
Remember, they are literal embodiments of corrosion.
As you said, they cannot be meaningfully analysed even up-close.
Or, rather, you CAN, but what you perceive changes slightly each time.
You also cannot capture them, and they don't leave wrecks behind when destroyed. It's as though they don't physically exist.
In a way, they don't. They're shadows projected from a different dimension. Imperfectly, at that.
The data loss caused by the projection process is in fact the reason why they appear as blurs you can't perceive properly.
Think of them as corrupted data. The fact that they're there means the space you're in is being corroded.
Now, it looks like we're dealing with... What? The Revolutionary Front?!
Listen up! Those entities come from the data present in that space!
What do you mean?
Just tune in to the frequency we just sent you! Their fleet is broadcasting a message to the island!
This is the Revolutionary Front's AO Sector Fleet. We hereby issue an ultimatum to Samos Island.
Hand over the individuals behind the attack on the freighter Poltava, disarm your Enforcers, and immediately destroy all artificial intelligence laboratories on the island.
If our demands are not met in three hours, we will achieve these objectives by force.
It is apparent that Samos Island has become a breeding ground for corruption owing to the Oceanic Federation's policy of neglect.

It is home to experiments – especially on weaponized artificial intelligence – that have never received safety assessments nor been held to ethics guidelines, all in the name of "scientific progress."
These people have put guns in the hands of robots and given the power over life and death to computer programs.
It is exactly that which led to this tragedy. You cannot entrust human lives to machines.
Artificial intelligences armed with weapons are both uncontrollable and extremely dangerous, and the attack on the Poltava proved this.
If these evil machines are left to roam free, it will be the end of humanity.
(Are they serious? They'll capture the island by force if necessary?)
(They mentioned artificial intelligences and Enforcers... That means the Antiochus, Dr. Aoste's project.)
(Then there was that attack on a freighter. They were vague on details...)
(Seems safe to assume that the Antiochus struck the Poltava and hurt innocent people.)
(In other words, they threatened the safety of the world's oceans, prompting the Revolutionary Front to retaliate.)
Miss D, can you remember what this memory is about?
No, not a thing! But it's obviously bad! Bad and I don't like it!
...We are in agreement there.
Sounds like both the Eagle Union and the Royal Navy are involved with this island.
Those demands were pretty crazy... They want the scientists to hand over those people, disarm their units, and destroy their lab all in three hours.
You could do maybe two of those in that time, but all three? No way.
I think they were going to use force anyway all along.
BEEP BEEP!
I see you on the security cams, Commander! What are you doing on Samos?
What the–?!
(Shh! I'll handle this!)
Ahem. Dr. Anzeel?
Yes, that's my name! Aoste already bailed, so why are you back here?
Do you know about the attack on the Poltava? The Revolutionary Front is on the island's doorstep, ready to enact revenge for it.
Yes, I know about the thing that's filled the world's headlines for days now!
Do you seriously believe that? That the Antiochus would defy protocol and attack a civilian freighter passing by?
(That sounds just like what the Sirens we know would do...)
(Considering everything we've witnessed, that definitely isn't beyond the realm of possibility... as much as it pains me to say that to her.)
"Revenge," my ass... That's just what they want you to think.
The attack was a false flag by the Revolutionary Front. They wanted an excuse to send a fleet to Samos.
Laplace's Demon is nearly finished, and they want to steal it.
(Laplace's Demon?)
If we adopt this plan, there may be a chance of success.
However, we must ensure that the jamming device remains operational as long as possible. In the meantime, we'll have to hold the line while evacuations are carried out.
In other words...
We already know this, Dr. Aoste. We've taken it upon ourselves to build upon this framework.
...I understand. But even if that is the case, everyone has already sustained heavy losses. The actual feasibility of the timeframe you provided must still be carefully evaluated...
If the "Light of Dawn" is ever extinguished, then Laplace's Demon will cease to be. If we have not reached the evacuation threshold at that time, our future operational plans will be impacted.
Worry not. I will also help buy time for you.
Ahem. Dr. Anzeel?
I've already issued too many communications through the process of commanding the operation, and they've likely already identified me for destruction.
As long as I can continue to draw their attention, I should be able to alleviate the pressure in other places, giving you more time to implement the plan.
But, what will happen to you then?
After such a fiasco, someone always has to bear the responsibility. Only now, there aren't many people left who can shoulder that burden.
I see... You can bring your KAN-SEN along as well. However, I would like Code G to accompany my Antiochus if possible.
Very well. You can have Enterprise, and whoever else manages to survive.
However, take them as far away from NY City as you can. Never let them return here.
...Without you here, I'm not sure I'll be able to control them...
What happened to the ever-so-wise and stoic Magister? You have no choice but to give those children both a purpose and the responsibility that comes with it, right?

...Even after you "betrayed" us, I still believe we see things eye-to-eye. And I think what you're doing is just. I completely understand you, and I trust that in their hearts, they understand you as well...
I'm sure they'll need some time to accept it, but they will come to understand you one day...
Yes, I'm sure of it...
(That's what I heard when I was in the Arcana Sanctum. Is this what that conversation referred to?)
So the Revolutionary Front wants to steal your time machine – Laplace's Demon. Does the Federation know about this?
Of course it does. They got this far in the first place because the RF had the Federation's tactic permission.
The Federation wants the RF to make the first move and escalate tensions. That way, they don't have to take the blame for starting a war.
Wait... Starting a war?
Yeah. A war that will erase them from time.

options.content: They're going to start a World War...

Laplace's Demon.say: Seize the power to control time, and the world shall be yours.
Don't freak out. Aoste and I already have a plan.
Flare – as in, Enterprise and company – will swoop in unannounced and steal Laplace's Demon and make both sides take a chill pill.
Enterprise? You mean... Code G?!
Look, we've chatted enough and this island is about to become a warzone! You need to get out of here NOW!
The communicator falls silent.
(The tension between the two major players in the alpha timeline is about to boil over into all-out war.)
(But, if Anzeel's and Aoste's plan is a success and Laplace's Demon falls into neither side's hands, then this memory won't have anything to do with death.)
(So what in the world is going to happen here?)
Commaaander. Who was that? Do you know her?
According to the memory we're in, yes. Why do you ask?
Because... she contacted you using the Tribunal's private frequency.
Everyone on this mission, including Elizabeth, is also using it.
This frequency is from OUR timeline, so how did that woman know about it and call us on it like it was the most natural thing in the world?
(That's a good point... I've grown so used to our frequencies being jammed or intercepted that I've stopped questioning it.)
How she did it doesn't matter!
Didn't you hear her? We need to get out of here, and fast!
It's too late for that. If this memory really is a representation of death, Anzeel's and Aoste's plan is going to fall through.
Huh? How do you know that?
Because if it had succeeded, this space wouldn't exist in the first place.

Flare's Past.say: In the midst of the raging storm, the fleet known as the Flare sails for Samos Island.
Aw, man. It was sunny just a little while ago. Why'd it have to go and rain on us?
The weather's so fickle around Samos Island, I swear.
The abnormal weather and sea conditions here are side effects of research on Laplace's Demon.
Sometimes, people even report ghost and monster sightings.
Is it that bad? That's a little spooky...
Kasumi wants to see them.
With you around, we might just end up meeting some ghosts.
I'd say this storm comes with a silver lining. It makes it easier for us to hide our maneuvers.
The higher-ups put too much faith in Laplace's Demon. They went and stirred up a crisis just because it's "almost done."
And for what? Because they think if things come to all-out war, they can win, and it won't matter? As if anything could be that easy.
They're not the only crazy ones. The RF came out in full force just to steal the incomplete machine.
I can't say I don't empathize. If they waited until it was complete and things went the way the Federation wants, it would already be too late.
You do have a point!
"Laplace's Demon is meant to serve all mankind. Not as a tool of war, nor as the trigger that ushers the destruction of mankind."
We have to stop them.
Of course! In fact, why don't we take it a step further?
Thanks to Dr. Anzeel, we already know her location and the status of the defenses near her.
Why don't we go rescue her, while we're at it?
Rescuing her is one thing. What happens after that? Will she actually flee with us?
That would be unnecessary. Dr. Anzeel is sure to have a plan.
Yeah! Even when she was imprisoned, she told us not to come to her rescue. She's just under house arrest right now, so we don't have to go out of our way.
Besides, the more of a mess we make out here, the more dangerous things will be for Dr. Anzeel and the people confined with her.
Ooh... My bad!
Enterprise, the Revolutionary Front has issued an ultimatum to Samos Island.
The Federation's ambush fleet has also arrived at their target coordinates.
...Sounds like it's time for us to act.
Wichita, what's the problem? You're awfully quiet today... You nervous, or something?
Ahahaha, nah, I've just got a lot on my mind. I'll go scout the area with Kimberly again, try to get my head on straight.
Alright. Don't stray too far from the main fleet.
Duh. Don't you worry about me!
...Nobody should hear us around here.
The beginning of the end... I didn't think we'd be brought back to this day.
I can tell that this is the new space manifested by the Martyrium.
But why today? Why is this what it manifested?
Our concept of death must have mixed into the formula when we entered.
No, to take it even further, maybe our concept of death is so strong that it superseded the beta timeline's perception of the Iris ruins...
Heh. So that means we died today, huh? Hahahaha!
Wichita.
Ah, sorry. That was meant to be a joke.
Can you trace that META Hatakaze?
She's in this space, but I can't trace her exact coordinates. She must be in hiding.
But there's no doubt that this space is corroded. The RF and the Federation are all corrosion entities.
Aha. So that was the point of that whole suicide shtick.
Wichita, do you remember that day? Corrosion phenomena appeared back then, too.
We didn't know anything, so we only called them black tornadoes.
If we can learn the things we overlooked back then, we might just be able to dig out new truths from these fixed memories.
This is a big opportunity.
Heh. We can't change what really transpired, but what about righting the wrongs of the past in our memories?
If we did things differently, made better choices back then...
Could we have made a better future?
There's no way to know now, but I certainly don't want to see that tragedy a second time.
Agreed. Let's do something about it.

The Stars in the Night Sky.say: Samos Island - Shortly before
One does question one thing.
If Laplace's Demon is such an important machine, why don't they simply take it elsewhere for protection?
Who knows? Maybe it's too big to carry that easily?
If the plan is for Flare to steal it, then it should be small enough to carry in one's personal storage, no?
If shipgirls can do it, then surely the Antiochus can, too...
Simply stealing it is one thing. The way out is fraught with danger.
Perhaps the best way to see a well-protected target transported to a safer location is to let it be stolen without a fight.
That might be the doctor's exact plan.
So how do we fit into all this?
Do we contact the Revolutionary Front, tell them it's a Federation trap, and hope they give up the fight?
I doubt that would help. The completion of Laplace's Demon is a life-or-death problem for the RF.
Trap or not, they can't afford to back out that easily.
Besides... I don't think they're likely to trust us.
I know! Let's call Aoste and have him hide Laplace's Demon!
Absolutely not. That would cement them as enemies of the Federation.
Further, if he could hide it without anyone's knowledge, he wouldn't need to get Flare to steal it.
It's starting to look like we only have one option.
Oh! Are you gonna kick both of their butts at once, Commander?
Fleur, that's obviously not the plan!
You're going to try and get your hands on Laplace's Demon first, aren't you?
That's right. Leaving things as they are means that the status quo stays intact – this space is engulfed by the concept of death. Therefore, our interference is what'll have to change things.
(This is only assuming that Flare failed to steal it, though.)
(If the problem lies in the doctors' method of salvaging the situation, then...)
(No, now isn't the time to hesitate. It's time for action.)
Shortly after...
We've approached the Samos Marine Research Institute with Miss D in tow.
Apart from automated sentries, they ran into no security staff along the way.
That alone reinforces Dr. Anzeel's Federation false flag claims.
KABOOOM!
The sound of battle?
It's been three hours already. The RF and Antiochus must be fighting by now, right?
You're close to Laplace's Demon. Hurry and secure it before Flare can.
Hm? Wait, that's...
(That's the META Wichita. So they ended up here too, did they?)
(Now that I think about it, they might be the ones this memory belongs to... Takao, Enterprise...)

options.content: Were the Ashes originally Flare all along?

The Stars in the Night Sky.say: (The Flare burns out, and all that's left is Ashes...)
Well, that's one way to greet someone.
Little late to figure that out. You're right, but not all the way there.
The survivors of Flare are among the Ashes, yes... But as the name implies, we're very literally what was left behind.
The fact that you'd press on, even knowing that, means that you're still totally clueless.
Are you and Kimberly the ones who experienced this event in the alpha timeline?
...I have nothing to say on that matter.
Sorry. Let me change the subject, then.
I got a call from Dr. Anzeel. She told me the RF and Federation's plans, along with what Flare is trying to do.
The doctor contacted you?!
I don't know why she would contact YOU, but... she's under strict surveillance. She had to move mountains just to contact us.
Contacting you specifically, and from Samos...
No, if she told you Flare's plan, too, then that's all the more suspicious...
So? What are you planning? Going to steal Laplace's Demon before Flare gets to it?
Yeah. We know that leaving this situation to play out normally would result in the death of this whole area.
And personally, I don't want Flare getting involved in this, either.
You're a weird one... but alright. I'm with you there.
Don't worry. Flare isn't coming.
They're not?
Yeah, they're not. We already got them all out of the area to keep them away from what's about to happen.
Did you and Kimberly come to steal Laplace's Demon, too, then?
Hahaha. Would you believe me if I told you that NOBODY got anywhere near the infernal machine on that day?
...It's almost time.
If you're so curious about it, then come get a front-row seat. You won't want to miss this.
We canceled our plans and watched the distant sea along with the two Ashes.
The Revolutionary Front already broke through the Enforcers' defensive line, and their vanguard appeared on the horizon.
Just then, the Federation forces reappeared from the sea below and skies above.
According to our long-range reconnaissance, more Federation forces were moving to surround them from three sides.
Neither fleet fired the first shot, but neither did they cease their forward march.
On that day, Enterprise brought us here.
The Marine Research Institute was right in front of us. All we had to do was steal Laplace's Demon, and this absurd situation would be resolved.
And then... this happened.
The world begins to darken, as if a solar eclipse has begun. The sky above distorts, bends, and twists, taking the shape of a massive vortex.
A black tornado appears, whipping up the sea and growing larger with every passing moment.
The black tornado... A corrosion phenomenon.
There you go. Yes, it's a corrosion phenomenon.
Back then, we didn't know what it was either. We called it a black tornado, just like you.
Heh. Yeah, no warning signs at all. It appears right out of nowhere.
Commander, the tornado is rapidly spreading! It's going to engulf us soon!
Stare at the pretty colours all you want, but that's a corrosion phenomenon, Commander! You won't survive long in there!
Flee at once, or else!
With a deafening roar, the whale jumps out of the ocean and disappears into the black clouds.
Aaargh! My whale got away again! Elizabeth, hurry up and anchor that concept!
We're doing that already! Wait just one minute, would you?!
Okay! Everyone else, gather around me! We're getting the hell outta here!
Heh, good idea. You'll want to get far away from this danger zone.
Aren't you coming? Corrosion must be dangerous for META, too.
We were weak back then. So we panicked, and we overlooked some important things.
We're different now.
We'll stay here and bear witness to the end.
Then I'm staying, too. Miss D, take the others to the next ring for me!
Do you hear yourself, assistant?!
Commander?!
Swordbearer, it's too dangerous!
I'll be fine. If I use the concept of death instead of the train, I can catch up to you!
That's how you got here, isn't it, Wichita?
Right. But...
Even if you accept death and make it before you're corroded, death hurts! It hurts to death!
Maybe, but there's so much to gain. It might even be as important as your whale.
D-dang... Okay. Good luck, assistant!
Wait! One should stay... to protect you from the worst case!
I'll stay, too!
M-me, too!
I'll be just fine, everyone. Have faith in Miss D, Elizabeth, and me!
...Order received...
Once Elizabeth gave the signal that her preparations were complete, Miss D and the Iris shipgirls all disappeared.
All that remain next to me are the future Wichita and Kimberly.
...Answer a few questions for me. How do you know so much?
Our secrets, the secrets of the Antiochus... It's as if you know all of it...
Why do we feel such nostalgia toward you?
Why couldn't Enterprise do away with you?
Why... can't I do it, either?
Who in the world are you?
Honestly, Kimberly, I could use an answer to that myself.
That's why I'm here with you right now.
...Okay.
Then let's watch the end unfold... together.
No more words are shared between us. We all watch in silence as the predetermined end comes.
The black vortex grows in strength and ferocity in an attempt to swallow everything.
Then, as if a critical point has been reached, a shockwave erupts from the center.
Everything within view turns black.
Wichita and Kimberly have already fallen. My body and mind creak under the weight of it, rapidly approaching the point of collapse.
My hazy mind, on the verge of melting entirely, is enveloped in blue foam.
I see the blackened sky. Fire, smoke.
A single shooting star streaks through the desolation.
How beautiful they are, the stars in the night sky.

\"My\" End.say: Is this... that same space again?
Miss D says I got lost when I came in here, but... I think this might be the narrow gap between life and death.
(I must've been brought here after I'd died – before the black tornado could corrode me.)
The foam still covers my mind, protecting my "self".
...Back again, "I" see.

options.content: Am I the only one here?

\"My\" End.say: A meaningless question.
Nowhere.
Yes.
It's "my" turn to ask the questions now.
Do you know that what you've been witnessing are mere images?
Foregone conclusions, all illusions, all destined to fade away.
Yeah. I know that very well.
Why struggle? Why resist? Why suffer and face death?
What meaning is there to all of it?
Well... It's something only I can do. Doesn't matter if it's real or not.
I follow my heart, stick to my sincere beliefs, and do what I must do.
What does the meaning matter, as long as I understand it? If I must, I can share it with someone else.
I think there's inherent meaning in treading a path you believe in.
Even if it seems meaningless at a glance, nobody knows what effects it might lead to.
...Answer received.
You're still under the illusion that you can change established reality.
Recently, somebody told me, "Reality and truth are not always one and the same. Reality can only be truth if enough faith goes into it."
I've spent a long time trying to figure out what that means...
But now, it makes perfect sense.
What? That all vain effort will be rewarded?
No. It means that you can't always accept reality as the truth.
If something doesn't exist in my memory, then it may be truth to others, but it isn't necessarily truth to me.
Reality may be set in stone for all I care, but if you don't accept it as the truth, then it isn't your truth.
No matter the situation, no matter the established premise...
Until I, as an individual, experience something, make decisions, and see things to their conclusion...
I won't accept it as the truth.
So I'm going to fight for the sake of my truth.
If that sounds like a vain effort to you, well, too bad. That's not enough reason for me to give up.
When the light fades, I see the shadows of trees.
The warm light shining through the leaves, the scent of flowers, the sounds of baby birds... An incredibly peaceful scene.
Next to me on this bench is a girl dressed in the style of the Royal Navy. In the space between us sits a small box of sweets.
Mmm... This is such a nice place.
Do you know where we are?
You're asking "me"?
This is a place that you constructed, that you interpreted, that you wished for.
It is your "end."
My... end?
I don't understand. What happened, and how did I end up here?
You don't, but "I" do.
You must go.
Go back to your battlefield. Continue your vain struggle.
"I'll" be watching you, Commander.

The Call That Never Happened.say: Light paints my vision white again. Instantly after, I see the dark of nighttime.
The roar of an engine reaches my ears. My nose is assaulted by the combined stench of smoke and death.
(Is this a battlefield? But I'm on... a mass-produced ship, aren't I?)
Aaaaaack! Where the bloody hell did you come from?!
Elizabeth? Weren't you on the Queen's Light?
Queen's Light...? Bel! Apprehend this stranger at once!
Understood, Your Majesty. *cough*...
Argh, fine! You just sit there and rest. We will take care of this ourselves!
(Everyone's acting weird...)
(Actually, this might just be another memory like the Samos Island. And if Elizabeth is right before my eyes...)
(...My communicator isn't reaching the Elizabeth in the train, after all. I guess there can only be one at a time in here.)
(Belfast is injured. This isn't a very reassuring situation.)
BEEP BEEP!
Your Majesty, the X fleet has blocked our retreat route.
...What kind, and how many?
A Class-V mimic beast, over a thousand mimic entities, and a vector zone of five square nautical miles.
We're struggling to handle a single Class-V? Gah, if only our fleet were in better condition...
Set course for a detour. We mean to avoid unnecessary combat.
Yes, Your Majesty.
Your Majesty. Enemies approach from all angles. We have no time for detours.
I can handle a Class-V mimic beast alone. Given the losses your fleet has suffered, I would suggest leaving it in my hands.
...Even if we can break through, the casualties will be enormous.
Here's the plan. We will bring up the rear. While we distract the enemy, the rest of you can detour and escape.
Is this a roundabout means of suicide?
No. The responsibility for this devastating defeat lies on our shoulders alone.
Please... Allow us to make amends for it.
If I may, I think you're only trying to run away. Your death would do nothing to improve the overall situation.
But you already know that, I'm sure.
We had a fleet of two hundred ships and one hundred KAN-SEN. Now, we're down to less than thirty percent...
The other fronts in this battle don't fare much better...
Tch... We put so much time and effort into gathering that force.
We don't want to see anyone else die...
Your Majesty, this is doomed to fail.
The Royal Navy would never abandon you.
I'm sorry, Your Majesty, but we won't accept those orders.
*cough*...
Hey, you can't predict the enemy's strength with any certainty. It's not your fault...
Don't give up, Your Majesty! You promised we'd make it home together!
With these numbers, I think we can make it through with a casualty rate of thirty percent. Detouring could buy us time, but it could also draw us deeper into a corner...
The Royal Navy stands and fights.
No matter how dire the circumstances, we must face them together, Your Majesty.
Yeah. Let's break through this encirclement together!
BEEP BEEP!
A communicator beeps again – not Elizabeth's, but mine this time.
Comma... Ahem. Assistant, thank goodness I've finally reached you.
Dr. Anzeel... right?
Did you say Dr. Anzeel?!
E-Elizabeth? Why are you with my assistant?
Hm? The person who dropped in here out of nowhere was your ASSISTANT?
Yep.
...We will accept that as truth. Anzeel, how are things with the Council?
Everyone's safe here. Some of the lost fleets are arriving at the rendezvous point.
That's a relief...
You'd better get your butt back here too, okay?
We... are currently planning our escape.
We've strayed too far from our allies, though, so the odds are... not in our favour.
Elizabeth, don't give up yet. Hope is right there by your side.
Can I get you to surrender leadership of the battle to my assistant there?
Surrender leadership? To this stranger? Have you lost your mind?
Don't worry. I'm certain my assistant – the Commander – will lead you to victory.
...Fine, fine, we trust you. Of course, we're supposed to obey your orders to begin with.
Alright, Commander, go wild. Reinforcements are on the way.
I'm waiting for you to come home to us.
Dr. Anzeel? Wait, you're...
Hm? Were you saying something?
Don't worry about it. You be careful.
No need to worry about me. We're in a safe place.
Also, don't stray away from the flying battleships. And avoid contact with the X at all costs.
Hanging up now.
(Hmm. The way this Anzeel talks, I think I know who she really is.)
(So in this memory, Dr. Anzeel and I were totally absent.)
(That means, if we advance as we are, only death and destruction await...)
Anzeel has awfully high hopes for you. Go on, then – show us what you can do.
What will your first order be, Commander?
I need a damage report on all ships and info on what weapons, personnel, and resources are available to us.
In the meantime, all ships who can fight will head to the first defensive line. Put mass-produced ships on the second defensive line. We're planting our feet firmly.
Planting our feet? But the battle lines are a total mess. And we don't know if reinforcements really...
Anzeel said there are reinforcements. Trust her.
Fight until the last light has faded.
Yes, Your Majesty.
My orders are based on intuition, but all ships quickly spring into action.
From the flying battleship that I'd only read of in Guam's reports, I gaze upon the dark sea.
In the distance, a great storm brews.
Beneath the storm, a fluid emitting an uncanny blue light coats the sea.
Countless things seem to stagger, squirm, and crowd upon it.
So this is the alpha timeline's enemy... One that we'll be forced to face in our world's future.
You've finally shown yourselves... "X".
